name: TimeWarp-a-tron

consumes.minimum: 1 active comp + 1 layer with time remapping enabled (canSetTimeRemapEnabled = true) + 1 controller layer (any layer that can host effects)
consumes.description: Reads the active CompItem. The 'pick time-remap' button reads selectedLayers[0] and checks canSetTimeRemapEnabled, then stores the layer's 'ADBE Time Remapping' property. The 'pick controller' button reads selectedLayers[0] and selectedProperties[0]; it searches the controller layer's effect parade for an existing named slider or a selected ADBE Slider Control. Reads the composition frameRate at OK-click time to embed in the generated expression.

controls.name: pickTimeRemapButton
controls.role: captures the currently selected layer as the time-remapped source; turns green (🟢) on success, alerts on invalid selection

controls.name: pickControllerButton
controls.role: captures the currently selected layer/effect as the slider controller; creates a new Slider Control effect if none exists (after confirm prompt); turns green (🟢) on success

controls.name: OK
controls.role: validates both picks, adds 'In/Out Range' Point Control to the time-remap layer, ensures slider on controller layer, builds and writes the linear time-remap expression, then closes the dialog

controls.name: Cancel
controls.role: closes the dialog without applying any changes

functions: wrap all mutations in a single undo group named 'TimeWarp-a-tron'
guard: alert if pickTimeRemapButton is clicked without a layer selected that supports time remapping
guard: alert if pickControllerButton is clicked before a time-remap layer has been picked
guard: alert if OK is clicked before both a time-remap layer and a controller layer have been picked
detect and store the 'ADBE Time Remapping' property from the selected time-remap layer
search the controller layer's effect parade by name for an existing named slider control
accept a pre-selected ADBE Slider Control effect on the controller layer without prompting
confirm-prompt the user to add a new Slider Control when no matching effect exists on the controller layer
add an ADBE Slider Control effect to the controller layer and name it '<layerName> || Time Control'
add an ADBE Point Control effect named 'In/Out Range' to the time-remap layer (if not already present)
set the 'In/Out Range' Point Control default value to [0, 60] (frame numbers for In/Out boundaries)
build a linear-interpolation expression string that maps a 0–100 slider value to a time range defined by the 'In/Out Range' point control divided by the composition frame rate
write the generated expression onto the layer's 'ADBE Time Remapping' property

scriptSim.scenario: link time-remap layer to new slider controller
scriptSim.inputs: Active comp 'VFX Comp' (1920×1080, 24fps, 10s); layer 'Footage Layer' (index 1, AV, has ADBE Time Remapping); layer 'Controller NULL' (index 2, Null, no effects); remap layer initially selected; user clicks pickTimeRemapButton, switches selection to 'Controller NULL', clicks pickControllerButton (no existing slider → confirm 'yes'), clicks OK

operations.target: No slider controller found. Would you like to add a Control Slider?

sim.note: Sweeps the Slider Control value from 0 (→ outputMin = inOutRange[0]/24 = 0s) to 100 (→ outputMax = inOutRange[1]/24 = 2.5s at 24fps with default [0,60]). Output is the linearly remapped time in seconds fed to the ADBE Time Remapping property, controlling playback position across the in/out frame range.

notes: Time remapping must be manually enabled on the source layer before running the script (canSetTimeRemapEnabled check is passive — the script does not enable it automatically).
The expression embeds the controller layer name and effect name as literal strings at generation time; renaming either layer or effect after application will break the expression.
The 'In/Out Range' Point Control stores frame numbers (not seconds); the expression divides by the composition frameRate (embedded as a literal at generation time) for AE time-in-seconds output.
The slider operates on a fixed 0–100 input domain regardless of the actual In/Out frame range; the range is entirely governed by the Point Control values.
If a slider effect whose matchName is 'ADBE Slider Control-0001' OR 'ADBE Slider Control' is pre-selected on the controller layer, it is used directly without renaming or confirmation.
The script searches for an existing controller by the name pattern '<layerName> || Time Control' before prompting to create a new one.
The endUndoGroup() call is placed after win.show() returns (modal dialog), so it fires after all user interactions including the OK click.
